commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh I will not reprove you for your sacrifices or your burnt offerings, to have been continually before me. I complain of no neglect (Menochius) in these outward ceremonies. (Haydock) God required no victims during the captivity; but he always demanded praise, (ver. ; Calmet) a contrite heart, Psalm l. (Haydock) The prophets often admonished the people of this truth, (Isaias i. , and Jeremias vii. ; Calmet) that they might not set too high a value on sacrifices, (Haydock) which, though pleasing to God, are of no service to him; as all the world is his property. (Worthington).
